name: rolling-deployment-with-health-checks
description: Validate that the skill produces a Kubernetes Deployment with proper rolling update strategy, all three probe types, resource management, and production hardening.
input: Create a Kubernetes Deployment manifest for a Node.js API service called 'order-api' in the 'commerce' namespace. Configure 3 replicas with a rolling update strategy (maxSurge: 1, maxUnavailable: 0). Add startup, readiness, and liveness probes hitting /healthz. Set resource requests (256Mi memory, 250m CPU) and limits (512Mi memory, 500m CPU). Add pod anti-affinity to spread replicas across nodes. Run as non-root user with a read-only root filesystem. Include a PodDisruptionBudget allowing at most 1 unavailable pod.

assertions.description: The Deployment must include a startup probe to handle slow container initialization without triggering premature liveness probe failures.
The Deployment must include a readiness probe so Kubernetes removes unhealthy pods from Service endpoints during rolling updates.
The rolling update strategy must specify maxSurge to control how many extra pods are created during deployment, managing cluster resource consumption.
The pod security context must enforce non-root execution to prevent privilege escalation attacks from within the container.
A PodDisruptionBudget must be defined to prevent voluntary disruptions (node drains) from evicting too many replicas simultaneously.

name: helm-chart-design
description: Validate that the skill produces a well-structured Helm chart with parameterized values, environment-specific overrides, and deployment best practices.
input: Create a Helm chart for a microservice called 'notification-service'. The chart should include: a Deployment template with configurable replicas, resources, and image tag; a Service (ClusterIP); an Ingress with TLS; a ConfigMap for application settings; a HorizontalPodAutoscaler scaling between 2-10 replicas based on CPU (70% target). Provide values.yaml with sensible defaults and a values-production.yaml override with higher replicas, stricter resources, and production domain.

assertions.description: Templates must use Helm value references for all configurable fields so that environment-specific overrides work without modifying templates.
The chart must include a HorizontalPodAutoscaler resource to enable automatic scaling based on resource utilization metrics.
A production values file must be provided with environment-specific overrides for replicas, resources, and domain configuration.
The Ingress resource must include TLS configuration to enforce encrypted traffic for production endpoints.
Application configuration must be externalized into a ConfigMap rather than hardcoded in the Deployment, enabling configuration changes without redeployment.
